description_long: This object is a large, wooden half-pipe structure commonly found in urban skate parks, featuring a smooth, curved ramp surface with vertical extensions at both ends. The exterior is adorned with colorful graffiti art, including stylized tags and murals on the side panels. The top edges are bordered by sturdy wooden railings for safety, and the ramp surface shows signs of wear and scuff marks from frequent use. The structure is elevated above the ground, with open space beneath the ramp and a flat platform at each end. The overall construction appears robust, with thick wooden panels and reinforced joints. The design is utilitarian, prioritizing function and durability for recreational activities.
description: A large, wooden half-pipe ramp with graffiti art, safety railings, and visible wear from use. The structure is robust and designed for recreational activities in urban environments.
description_view_0: Front view showing a vertical ramp face with prominent graffiti and wooden railings on top.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the full curved interior of the half-pipe, both platforms, and multiple graffiti artworks along the base.
description_view_2: Opposite end view with a different graffiti mural and similar railing structure.
description_view_3: Alternate side view displaying additional graffiti and the full length of the ramp's curve.
